original_prompt: Please add all the ingredients for the Chocolate Chip Cookies recipe from recipe-collection.docx to the grocery list I already have in the cloud drive.
assessment_reasoning: The prompt clearly states the source file (recipe-collection.docx), the recipe (Chocolate Chip Cookies), and the desired action (add all ingredients to the existing grocery list in the cloud drive). However it omits a specific target file identifier or exact grocery-list filename (could be multiple matches), does not specify how to format the added items (bullet style, newlines, prefixes), and leaves unstated behavior like deduplication or handling multiple recipes with similar names. These small unspecified execution details create slight ambiguity but the overall intent is clear.

variations.L1: Put the cookie recipe ingredients into my grocery list in the cloud.
variations.L2: Please add the ingredients for the Chocolate Chip Cookies recipe from my recipe collection to my grocery list in the cloud drive.
variations.L3: Open the file recipe-collection.docx, find the Chocolate Chip Cookies recipe, and add all its ingredients to my grocery list file in my cloud drive. Keep the existing items and just add the missing ingredients.
variations.L4: Please open recipe-collection.docx, locate the Chocolate Chip Cookies recipe, extract all ingredient lines, and append them to my existing grocery list file in the cloud drive. Do not remove or modify existing list items; add each ingredient as a new line prefixed with '- '.
variations.L5: Step-by-step: 1) Search for the file named 'recipe-collection.docx' (call search_files_by_filename with filename='recipe-collection.docx') and open it. 2) From that document extract the ingredient list for the recipe titled 'Chocolate Chip Cookies' as plain ingredient lines. 3) Search for my grocery list file (call search_files with query='grocery list') and choose the grocery list file returned. 4) Append the extracted ingredients to that grocery list by calling append_to_file with file_id=<grocery_list_file_id> and content set to the ingredients joined as '\n- ' (so each ingredient is added as a new line prefixed with '- '). Do not delete or alter existing content.
